[decision:dual-executor-policy] Subprocess workers are NOT abandoned. Current Windows subprocess path is paused only for blind retries after silent-runner-failure evidence; it remains a first-class executor to mature alongside SDK/in-process. SDK/in-process is a parallel governed fallback/alternative, not a replacement. Future policy should select executor by scenario: subprocess for stronger process isolation, argv/log reproducibility, external timeout/kill; SDK/in-process for direct event visibility, AgentSession control, abort/final-output introspection, or when subprocess startup is fragile. Devcontainer/Linux canary is a valid future subprocess maturity probe to distinguish Windows/path/entrypoint issues from runner/provider issues.

tasks.description: Investigate and harden auto-resume continuity after compact/reload suppression
acceptance_criteria: Record a reproducible incident packet when auto-compact/reload suppresses expected auto-resume and the operator must nudge manually.
Define a post-reload resume pending-state contract that survives compact/reload and explains why auto-resume did or did not fire.
Add/adjust a local smoke test or report-only guard so future repeated stops are visible before relying on unattended continuation.
Preserve explicit human-control gates: no scheduler, remote, or worker dispatch is authorized by the resume signal itself.
tasks.notes:
2026-05-12 incident: user observed forced auto-compact, then manual reload suppressed auto-resume; no auto-resume fired, requiring a manual nudge. Current status evidence showed `postReloadResume=pending`, stale handoff, dirty local slice, and continuation readiness blocked on candidate/checkpoint/handoff-budget. This should be hardened so unexplained stops are logged and resumed/blocked with operator-visible reason.
[provenance:human] source_task=TASK-BUD-1071 reason=Parallel/continuity discussion exposed a repeated operational failure mode: work stopped despite local-safe work remaining after compact/reload boundary.

Post-resume state was git clean, reload not required, but handoff stale and continuation readiness blocked (`candidate:invalid|checkpoint:invalid|stop-conditions:invalid`) despite local-safe TASK-BUD-1071 work remaining. Evidence: VERIF-TASK-BUD-1072-WEBSOCKET-1006-SESSION-RESUME-20260512.
2026-05-13: New continuity incident after forced compact/reload: auto-resume remained pending/suppressed and operator had to send a manual continuation message. `context_watch_status` on resume showed postReloadResume=pending, handoff stale age 8755/1800, level ok, reload-not-required. Evidence: VERIF-TASK-BUD-1072-POST-RELOAD-MANUAL-NUDGE-20260513.
2026-05-15 user feedback after forced compact/reload/restart: `npm run pi:dev:resume` reported the loop paused, but a stale/suppressed auto-resume still fired after return. Also reload after runtime changes appeared to take unbounded time; unclear whether pi/runtime hang or local machine slowness. Seeded TASK-BUD-1081 for resume/pause/auto-resume coherence and TASK-BUD-1082 for reload lifecycle diagnostics.
2026-05-22: Added a canonical report-only post-reload incident packet and tool surface. Pending auto-resume suppression now records a structured incident packet with reason, focus, age, operator-action cue, and explicit no-dispatch/no-mutation/no-remote/no-scheduler guarantees. Manual-nudge evidence can be captured through context_watch_post_reload_incident_packet without triggering resume.

tasks.description: Implement a P0 development velocity pressure primitive that detects when session/runtime/control-plane overhead is slowing development enough to require checkpoint, reduced governance surface, compact, new session, or cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Define a report-only velocity pressure packet with context, dirty state, machine pressure, runtime/process age, dangling process count, board/tool-call pressure, and time since useful commit signals.

The primitive must never kill processes, reload, compact, dispatch workers, mutate settings, or clean disk; it only recommends continue/checkpoint-and-commit/reduce-governance-surface/compact/new-session/block-and-clean.
Add a lightweight operator protocol for high pressure: stop feature work, validate current dirty scope, commit/revert/stash, write minimal handoff, and prefer a clean session when needed.
Cover the primitive with deterministic tests for context-only pressure, dirty-scope pressure, disk/memory pressure, dangling-process pressure, and excessive ceremony/tool-call pressure.
Keep the implementation local-first and avoid reading the full board unless explicitly requested.
